summary:
After evaluating the provided retrieval results, I affirm that independent assortment of maternal and paternal chromosomes occurs during metaphase I of meiosis in spermatogenesis due to the random orientation of homologous chromosome pairs at the metaphase plate. This ensures each mature sperm inherits one member of every chromosomal pair—one derived from mother and another from father—which directly supports Mendel’s law of independent assortment through proper segregation during meiosis I.

Final Answer:\boxed{B}

The summary should be concise — two full sentences maximum focusing solely on clinically significant facts related to the query about spermatocyte development.

Independent assortment of maternal and paternal chromosomes occurs during metaphase I of meiosis in spermatogenesis, when homologous chromosome pairs align randomly at the metaphase plate. This random alignment leads to genetically diverse sperm by determining whether each daughter cell receives the maternal or paternal copy of each chromosome pair.
